revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=draft}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=draft}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=draft}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969. * Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980. * History of Sakae Editorial Committee, 'History of Sakae,' Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2013. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center, '[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31],' 2015. * Yokohama City Board of Education, '[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City],' 2019. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.html Cultural Properties and Buried Cultural Assets] - Official page of the Yokohama City Board of Education * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City (PDF)] - Yokohama City Board of Education
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs == ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kannami|en}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Numazu|en}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=draft}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tamana|en}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Usa|en|12=draft}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.
*

'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=draft}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=draft}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=draft}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=draft}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=draft}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=draft}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969. * Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980. * History of Sakae Editorial Committee, 'History of Sakae,' Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2013. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center, '[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31],' 2015. * Yokohama City Board of Education, '[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City],' 2019. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.html Cultural Properties and Buried Cultural Assets] - Official page of the Yokohama City Board of Education * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City (PDF)] - Yokohama City Board of Education
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs == ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kannami|en}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Numazu|en}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs

'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

{{日本の古墳 |名称 =七石山横穴墓群 |別名 =七石山横穴古墳群 |画像 =[[File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs1.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション =七石山横穴墓群残存部 |所在地 =[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[栄区]]小菅ヶ谷1-2丁目 |所属 = [[㹨川流域横穴墓群]] |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 =県:栄区№21
市:栄区№34 |位置 = |緯度度 =35|緯度分 =22|緯度秒 =3.9 |経度度 =139|経度分 =32 |経度秒 =48.11 |ISO =JP-14 |形状 =[[横穴墓]] |築造年代 =[[7世紀]]末から[[8世紀]] |埋葬施設 =奥室構造を持つ「鍛冶ヶ谷式」横穴 |規模 = |被葬者 =不明 |出土品 =人骨・鉄製品・[[須恵器]]・玉類など |史跡 = [[横浜市登録地域文化財|横浜市地域史跡]] |出土品の文化財指定 =なし |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|label=七石山
横穴墓群|lat_deg=35|lat_min=22|lat_sec=3.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=32|lon_sec=48.11|mark=横穴墓.svg|marksize=30|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=横穴墓群の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|22|3.9|N|139|32|48.11|E}}}} }} '''七石山横穴墓群'''（しちこくやまおうけつぼぐん/しちこくやまよこあなぼぐん）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[栄区]][[小菅ヶ谷]]1-2丁目にある[[横穴墓]]群。約20群200基以上あった'''[[㹨川流域横穴墓群]]'''の1支群である埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1～2ページ。かつてJR[[根岸線]]の[[本郷台駅]]周囲に100基以上あったとみられ、正確に調査されただけで43基存在したが、現在は4基ほどが線路北側に残るにすぎない。1988年（昭和63年）に[[横浜市登録地域文化財|横浜市地域史跡]]に登録されている（登録名称は「七石山横穴古墳群（しちこくやまよこあなこふんぐん）」）{{Sfn|横浜市教育委員会|2019}}。 == 概要 == [[㹨川]]流域をはじめとする[[南関東]]の[[丘陵]]部辺縁には、掘削しやすい土質の傾斜が多く、横穴墓が多く築造された。 [[1967年]]（昭和42年）に[[根岸線]]敷設工事に伴って現・本郷台駅周辺の[[丘陵]]が大規模に削られた際、横浜市郷土研究会会員の千貝幸之助（ちがいこうのすけ）により存在が通報され、[[1968年]]（昭和43年）{{Sfn|横浜市文化財研究調査会|1969}}、[[1970年]]（昭和45年）、[[1972年]]（昭和47年）{{Sfn|明星大学考古学研究部|1980}}に[[発掘調査]]が実施された。 特徴は、近隣の地名をとって「[[鎌倉型横穴墓]]（[[鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]）」とよばれるアーチ型の断面を持ち、よく発達した[[玄室]]の奥に小型の「棺室」をもつ復室構造であること、[[羨道]]は存在しないか、またはかなり退化していることが挙げられる栄区地域振興課『栄の歴史』（2013年）20～21ページ。人骨のほか、[[土師器]]・[[須恵器]]類、[[勾玉]]・[[管玉]]類、[[剣|鉄剣]]・[[鉄刀]]類、[[耳環]]などが出土している。年代観としては[[古墳時代]]終末期から[[奈良時代]]までに幅広く編年され、継起的に造営されたことをうかがわせる。この[[地域]]の有力[[首長]]層、富裕[[農民]]層らの[[墳墓]]と考えられている。 ==ギャラリー== File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|横穴墓の現状。山が背後から削られたため、[[玄室]]の奥壁が無くなっている。 file:HongodaiSt.jpg|マンション建設前の[[本郷台駅]]前（2006年）。かつて本郷台はこのような[[丘陵]]地帯で、たくさんの[[横穴墓]]があった。 file:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|2014年の[[本郷台駅]]前。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==参考図書== *{{Cite journal|和書|author=横浜市文化財研究調査会|title=横浜市戸塚区七石山横穴古墳群及滝ケ久保横穴古墳群の調査|journal=七石山遺跡調査報告書|issue=1|publisher=横浜市文化財研究調査会|year=1969|date=1969|ref=harv}} *{{Cite journal|和書|author=明星大学考古学研究部|title=長谷久保遺跡・七石山横穴群|journal=明星大学考古学研究部研究報告|issue=1|publisher=[[明星大学]]|year=1980|date=1980|ref=harv}} *栄の歴史編集委員会『栄の歴史』栄区地域振興課 2013年（平成25年）3月発行 *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行 *{{Cite web|和書|author=横浜市教育委員会|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|title=国・神奈川県および横浜市指定・登録文化財目録|publisher=横浜市|year=2019|accessdate=2019-11-05|ref=harv}} 文化財・埋蔵文化財] 横浜市教育委員会公式ページ * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf 国・神奈川県および横浜市指定・登録文化財目録（PDF形式）]横浜市教育委員会公式ページ {{考古学}} {{日本の文化財}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969. * Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980. * History of Sakae Editorial Committee, 'History of Sakae,' Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2013. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center, '[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31],' 2015. * Yokohama City Board of Education, '[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City],' 2019. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.html Cultural Properties and Buried Cultural Assets] - Official page of the Yokohama City Board of Education * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City (PDF)] - Yokohama City Board of Education
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs == ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kannami|en}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Numazu|en}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tamana|en}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Usa|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}

'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969. * Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980. * History of Sakae Editorial Committee, 'History of Sakae,' Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2013. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center, '[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31],' 2015. * Yokohama City Board of Education, '[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City],' 2019. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.html Cultural Properties and Buried Cultural Assets] - Official page of the Yokohama City Board of Education * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City (PDF)] - Yokohama City Board of Education
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs == ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiya Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs|ja|法皇山横穴古墳|1=Hōōzan Cave Tombs|2=ja|3=法皇山横穴古墳|4=fr|5=Tombeaux troglodytes de Hōōzan|6=simple|7=Hōōzan Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōōzan Cave Tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōōzan Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenami Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukuhashi|qq=}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizumachi Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōgata|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozuki Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tamana|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamana|qq=}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga, Kumamoto|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoyoshi|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Usa|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa|qq=}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969. * Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980. * History of Sakae Editorial Committee, 'History of Sakae,' Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2013. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center, '[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31],' 2015. * Yokohama City Board of Education, '[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City],' 2019. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}

<!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster-->

'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group]] → [[Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs]]
revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969. * Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980. * History of Sakae Editorial Committee, 'History of Sakae,' Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2013. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center, '[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31],' 2015. * Yokohama City Board of Education, '[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City],' 2019. == See Also == * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.html Cultural Properties and Buried Cultural Assets] - Official page of the Yokohama City Board of Education * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City (PDF)] - Yokohama City Board of Education
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs == ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiya Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs|ja|法皇山横穴古墳|1=Hōōzan Cave Tombs|2=ja|3=法皇山横穴古墳|4=fr|5=Tombeaux troglodytes de Hōōzan|6=simple|7=Hōōzan Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōōzan Cave Tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōōzan Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenami Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukuhashi|qq=}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizumachi Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōgata|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozuki Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tamana|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamana|qq=}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga, Kumamoto|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoyoshi|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Usa|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa|qq=}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}

<!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster-->

'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969. * Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980. * History of Sakae Editorial Committee, 'History of Sakae,' Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2013. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center, '[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31],' 2015. * Yokohama City Board of Education, '[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City],' 2019. == See Also == * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]
== External Links == * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.html Cultural Properties and Buried Cultural Assets] - Official page of the Yokohama City Board of Education * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City (PDF)] - Yokohama City Board of Education
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs == ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
