'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:栄区の歴史
revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969. * Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980. * History of Sakae Editorial Committee, 'History of Sakae,' Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2013. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center, '[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31],' 2015. * Yokohama City Board of Education, '[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City],' 2019. == See Also == * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]
== External Links == * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.html Cultural Properties and Buried Cultural Assets] - Official page of the Yokohama City Board of Education * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City (PDF)] - Yokohama City Board of Education

revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969. * Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980. * History of Sakae Editorial Committee, 'History of Sakae,' Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2013. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center, '[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31],' 2015. * Yokohama City Board of Education, '[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City],' 2019. == See Also == * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]
== External Links == * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.html Cultural Properties and Buried Cultural Assets] - Official page of the Yokohama City Board of Education * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City (PDF)] - Yokohama City Board of Education
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs == ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs

=== Hokuriku Region === * [[:en:Hōōzan Cave Tombs|Hōōzan Cave Tombs]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[Oki District]])

revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}

revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969. * Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980. * History of Sakae Editorial Committee, 'History of Sakae,' Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2013. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center, '[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31],' 2015. * Yokohama City Board of Education, '[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City],' 2019. == See Also == * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]
== External Links == * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.html Cultural Properties and Buried Cultural Assets] - Official page of the Yokohama City Board of Education * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City (PDF)] - Yokohama City Board of Education
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs == ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs

'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.

File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. <!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:栄区の歴史]] [[Category:横浜市の古墳]] [[Category:神奈川県内の市町村指定史跡]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.

National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa|qq=}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969. * Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980. * History of Sakae Editorial Committee, 'History of Sakae,' Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2013. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center, '[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31],' 2015. * Yokohama City Board of Education, '[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City],' 2019. == See Also == * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]
== External Links == * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.html Cultural Properties and Buried Cultural Assets] - Official page of the Yokohama City Board of Education * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City (PDF)] - Yokohama City Board of Education
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs == ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * [[:en:Kashiya Cave Tombs|Kashiya Cave Tombs]] ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * [[:en:Hōōzan Cave Tombs|Hōōzan Cave Tombs]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[Oki District]])

revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}

== External Links == * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.html Cultural Properties and Buried Cultural Assets] - Official page of the Yokohama City Board of Education * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City (PDF)] - Yokohama City Board of Education

revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969. * Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980. * History of Sakae Editorial Committee, 'History of Sakae,' Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2013. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center, '[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31],' 2015. * Yokohama City Board of Education, '[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City],' 2019. == See Also == * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]
== External Links == * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.html Cultural Properties and Buried Cultural Assets] - Official page of the Yokohama City Board of Education * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City (PDF)] - Yokohama City Board of Education
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs == ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs

=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs

revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and the [[8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and the [[:en:8th century|8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969. * Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980. * History of Sakae Editorial Committee, 'History of Sakae,' Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2013. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center, '[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31],' 2015. * Yokohama City Board of Education, '[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City],' 2019. == See Also == * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]
== External Links == * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.html Cultural Properties and Buried Cultural Assets] - Official page of the Yokohama City Board of Education * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City (PDF)] - Yokohama City Board of Education

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Sakae Ward, Yokohama
revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and the [[:en:8th century|8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969. * Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980. * History of Sakae Editorial Committee, 'History of Sakae,' Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2013. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center, '[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31],' 2015. * Yokohama City Board of Education, '[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City],' 2019. == See Also == * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]
== External Links == * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.html Cultural Properties and Buried Cultural Assets] - Official page of the Yokohama City Board of Education * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City (PDF)] - Yokohama City Board of Education
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs == ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * [[:en:Kashiya Cave Tombs|Kashiya Cave Tombs]] ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * [[:en:Hōōzan Cave Tombs|Hōōzan Cave Tombs]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenami Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukuhashi|qq=}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizumachi Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōgata|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tamana|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamana|qq=}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga, Kumamoto|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - revision.text:
'''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and the [[:en:8th century|8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[sue ware]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
