[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] [[ja:栄区]] [[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] [[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群]] [[ja:函南町]] [[ja:北江間横穴群]] [[ja:伊豆の国市]] [[ja:江浦横穴群]] [[ja:沼津市]] [[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] [[ja:加賀市]] [[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] [[ja:柏原市]] [[ja:安福寺横穴群]] [[ja:十王免横穴群]] [[ja:松江市]] [[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] [[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] [[ja:出雲市]] [[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] [[ja:知夫村]] [[ja:竹並遺跡]] [[ja:行橋市]] [[ja:城山横穴群]] [[ja:福智町]] [[ja:水町遺跡]] [[ja:直方市]] [[ja:古月横穴]] [[ja:鞍手町]] [[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] [[ja:玉名市]] [[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] [[ja:鍋田横穴群]] [[ja:山鹿市]] [[ja:大村横穴群]] [[ja:人吉市]] [[ja:四日市横穴群]] [[ja:宇佐市]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] [[ja:大分市]] [[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] [[ja:宮崎市]] [[ja:横穴墓]]
<!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:History of Sakae Ward]] [[Category:Kofun in Yokohama City]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Historic Sites in Kanagawa Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11353656}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and the [[:en:8th century|8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[:en:Negishi Line|Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[:en:dromos|dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and the [[:en:8th century|8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[:en:Negishi Line|Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[:en:dromos|dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and the [[:en:8th century|8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[:en:Negishi Line|Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[:en:dromos|dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and the [[:en:8th century|8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[:en:Negishi Line|Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[:en:dromos|dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and the [[:en:8th century|8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[:en:Negishi Line|Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[:en:dromos|dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and the [[:en:8th century|8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[:en:Negishi Line|Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[:en:dromos|dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}

[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] [[ja:栄区]] [[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] [[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群]] [[ja:函南町]] [[ja:北江間横穴群]] [[ja:伊豆の国市]] [[ja:江浦横穴群]] [[ja:沼津市]] [[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] [[ja:加賀市]] [[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] [[ja:柏原市]] [[ja:安福寺横穴群]] [[ja:十王免横穴群]] [[ja:松江市]] [[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] [[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] [[ja:出雲市]] [[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] [[ja:知夫村]] [[ja:竹並遺跡]] [[ja:行橋市]] [[ja:城山横穴群]] [[ja:福智町]] [[ja:水町遺跡]] [[ja:直方市]] [[ja:古月横穴]] [[ja:鞍手町]] [[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] [[ja:玉名市]] [[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] [[ja:鍋田横穴群]] [[ja:山鹿市]] [[ja:大村横穴群]] [[ja:人吉市]] [[ja:四日市横穴群]] [[ja:宇佐市]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] [[ja:大分市]] [[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] [[ja:宮崎市]] [[ja:横穴墓]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and the [[:en:8th century|8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[:en:Negishi Line|Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[:en:dromos|dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and the [[:en:8th century|8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[:en:Negishi Line|Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[:en:dromos|dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and the [[:en:8th century|8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[:en:Negishi Line|Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[:en:dromos|dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and the [[:en:8th century|8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[:en:Negishi Line|Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[:en:dromos|dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and the [[:en:8th century|8th century]]. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]], and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in [[:en:1967|1967]] during excavation work for the [[:en:Negishi Line|Negishi Line]]. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in [[:en:1968|1968]], [[:en:1970|1970]], and [[:en:1972|1972]].<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ([[:en:dromos|dromos]]).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of [[:en:Hongōdai Station|Hongōdai Station]] in 2014. </gallery>

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} and the {{ill|8th century|en|8th century|WD=Q8086|lt=8th century}}. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around {{ill|Hongōdai Station|en|Hongōdai Station|WD=Q844379|lt=Hongōdai Station}}, and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967}} during excavation work for the {{ill|Negishi Line|en|Negishi Line|WD=Q1194511|lt=Negishi Line}}. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}, {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970}}, and {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972}}.<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ({{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos}}).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of {{ill|Hongōdai Station|en|Hongōdai Station|WD=Q844379|lt=Hongōdai Station}} before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of {{ill|Hongōdai Station|en|Hongōdai Station|WD=Q844379|lt=Hongōdai Station}} in 2014. </gallery>
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969. * Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980. * History of Sakae Editorial Committee, 'History of Sakae,' Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2013. * Yokohama City Buried Cultural Properties Center, '[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31],' 2015. * Yokohama City Board of Education, '[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City],' 2019. == See Also == * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Cave Tomb Tomb Group|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}} ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Tomb Group|1=Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97636257}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.html Cultural Properties and Buried Cultural Assets] - Official page of the Yokohama City Board of Education * [https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City (PDF)] - Yokohama City Board of Education
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs == ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en|Kashiya Cave Tombs|WD=Q22118267|lt=Kashiya Cave Tombs}} ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs|en|Hōōzan Cave Tombs|WD=Q18339249|lt=Hōōzan Cave Tombs}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[:en:Oki District|Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenami Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukuhashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizumachi Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōgata|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozuki Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tamana|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamana|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga, Kumamoto|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoyoshi|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} and the {{ill|8th century|en|8th century|WD=Q8086|lt=8th century}}. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around {{ill|Hongōdai Station|en|Hongōdai Station|WD=Q844379|lt=Hongōdai Station}}, and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967}} during excavation work for the {{ill|Negishi Line|en|Negishi Line|WD=Q1194511|lt=Negishi Line}}. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}, {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970}}, and {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972}}.<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ({{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos}}).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.

File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of {{ill|Hongōdai Station|en|Hongōdai Station|WD=Q844379|lt=Hongōdai Station}} before apartment development (2006). <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353656]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} and the {{ill|8th century|en|8th century|WD=Q8086|lt=8th century}}. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around {{ill|Hongōdai Station|en|Hongōdai Station|WD=Q844379|lt=Hongōdai Station}}, and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967}} during excavation work for the {{ill|Negishi Line|en|Negishi Line|WD=Q1194511|lt=Negishi Line}}. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}, {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970}}, and {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972}}.<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ({{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos}}).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of {{ill|Hongōdai Station|en|Hongōdai Station|WD=Q844379|lt=Hongōdai Station}} before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of {{ill|Hongōdai Station|en|Hongōdai Station|WD=Q844379|lt=Hongōdai Station}} in 2014. </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} and the {{ill|8th century|en|8th century|WD=Q8086|lt=8th century}}. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around {{ill|Hongōdai Station|en|Hongōdai Station|WD=Q844379|lt=Hongōdai Station}}, and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967}} during excavation work for the {{ill|Negishi Line|en|Negishi Line|WD=Q1194511|lt=Negishi Line}}. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}, {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970}}, and {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972}}.<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ({{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos}}).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of {{ill|Hongōdai Station|en|Hongōdai Station|WD=Q844379|lt=Hongōdai Station}} before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of {{ill|Hongōdai Station|en|Hongōdai Station|WD=Q844379|lt=Hongōdai Station}} in 2014. </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.36775|N|139.546697|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (七石山横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Kosugaya, Sakae Ward, Yokohama City, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is one of the subgroups of the larger {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} and was registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} in 1988.<ref>Yokohama City Board of Education, 'List of Cultural Properties of Japan, Kanagawa Prefecture, and Yokohama City' (2019).</ref>
== Overview == The Shichikokuyama Tomb Group was constructed between the late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} and the {{ill|8th century|en|8th century|WD=Q8086|lt=8th century}}. It originally consisted of more than 100 tombs around {{ill|Hongōdai Station|en|Hongōdai Station|WD=Q844379|lt=Hongōdai Station}}, and over 200 tombs across 20 subgroups within the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|lt=Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkawa River Basin Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}}.<ref>Buried Cultural Properties Center (Yokohama City), 'Maibun Yokohama No. 31' (2015), pp. 1-2.</ref> However, due to urban development, only four tombs remain on the northern side of the railway tracks.
The tombs were discovered in {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967}} during excavation work for the {{ill|Negishi Line|en|Negishi Line|WD=Q1194511|lt=Negishi Line}}. The existence of the tomb group was reported by Kōnosuke Chigai, a member of the Yokohama City Local History Research Association. Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}, {{ill|1970|en|1970|WD=Q2474|lt=1970}}, and {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972}}.<ref>Yokohama City Cultural Property Research and Investigation Committee, 'Excavation Report on the Shichikokuyama and Takigakubo Cave Tomb Tomb Groups,' 1969.</ref><ref>Meisei University Archaeological Research Club, 'Hasekuboyama Site and Shichikokuyama Cave Tomb Tombs,' 1980.</ref>
The tombs are categorized as "{{ill|Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave Tombs|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-type rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}" (also known as "{{ill|Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave Tombs|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style rock-cut tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}"). These feature an arch-shaped cross-section, a well-developed [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a smaller "coffin chamber" at the back, and either a missing or significantly reduced entrance passage ({{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos}}).<ref>Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 'History of Sakae' (2013), pp. 20-21.</ref> Excavations uncovered human remains, [[hajiki]] and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery, [[magatama]], [[tube-shaped beads]], [[:en:Iron swords|Iron swords]], [[iron knives]], and [[:en:Earrings|Earrings]]. These findings suggest that the tombs were constructed continuously from the late [[Kofun period]] to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, serving as burial sites for local elite rulers and wealthy farmers.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Shichikokuyama tunnel tombs2.jpg|Current state of the tombs. The rear walls have been eroded due to land excavation. File:HongodaiSt.jpg|View of {{ill|Hongōdai Station|en|Hongōdai Station|WD=Q844379|lt=Hongōdai Station}} before apartment development (2006). The area was once a hilly landscape with many rock-cut tombs. File:JReast Hongodaistation 20140319.jpg|View of {{ill|Hongōdai Station|en|Hongōdai Station|WD=Q844379|lt=Hongōdai Station}} in 2014. </gallery>
